lesson.menu_desc: Travel Vocabulary & Polite Requests with Could I / Could you -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- Travel English I -- Travel Vocabulary & Polite Requests
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 10 -- Travel English I

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Daniel. I am calling about your business trip to Madrid for the contract signing. Here is your itinerary. Your flight leaves on Tuesday morning, so please get to the airport early. At check-in, ask for an aisle seat if you prefer one, and remember you have one bag to check, so keep your boarding pass and your passport ready. When you land, go through customs first, then take a taxi to the hotel. I made a reservation under your name, so at the front desk just confirm the booking and ask for a quiet room. The closing meeting is on Wednesday at ten. If anything changes, call me. Have a safe trip, and talk soon.

Hello, this is Simone. I am recording my travel checklist for the Madrid trip, so I do not forget anything. First, at the airport, I will check in and say: could I have an aisle seat, please? I have one bag, so I will ask them to check my luggage. Then I will keep my boarding pass safe and find the departure gate. After I land, I will go through customs and take a taxi. At the hotel front desk, I would like to confirm my reservation, and I will ask: could I have a quiet room and a late check-out? Finally, I will review my itinerary for the closing meeting on Wednesday. I think I am ready for the trip.

extra_audio.text: Daniel leaves a voice note with the itinerary for the Madrid trip. Simone packs her bag and gets to the airport early. At the check-in desk, Simone asks for an aisle seat and checks her luggage. She goes through customs and takes a taxi to the hotel. At the front desk, Simone confirms her reservation and asks for a quiet room.
